title: try statements with identical catch and/or finally blocks should be merged
description: "try" statements with identical "catch" and/or "finally" blocks should be mergedAnalyze your codeadaptability-distinctmaintainabilityCode SmellclumsyWhy is this an issue?When multiple, adjacenttrystatements have duplicatecatchand/orfinallyblocks, they should be merged to consolidate thecatch/finallylogic for cleaner, more readable code. Note that this applies even when there is intervening code outside anytryblock.Noncompliant code exampletry { DoTheFirstThing(a, b); } catch (InvalidOperationException ex) { HandleException(ex); } DoSomeOtherStuff(); try // Noncompliant; catch is identical to previous { DoTheSecondThing(); } catch (InvalidOperationException ex) { HandleException(ex); } try // Compliant; catch handles exception differently { DoTheThirdThing(a); } catch (InvalidOperationExcep
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Multiple try statements with identical catch blocks detected. Consider merging them into a single try block for cleaner code.

never_trigger_on: Try blocks with different exception types in catch
Try blocks with different exception handlers
logic.reasoning: When multiple adjacent try statements have duplicate catch and/or finally blocks, they should be merged to consolidate the exception handling logic. This makes the code cleaner and more readable. The rule detects patterns where two try-catch blocks handle the same exception type with similar handlers.

should_trigger: try { A(); } catch (IOException e) { Log(e); } try { B(); } catch (IOException e) { Log(e); }
try { First(); } catch (Exception ex) { Handle(ex); } try { Second(); } catch (Exception ex) { Handle(ex); }
should_not_trigger: try { A(); B(); } catch (IOException e) { Log(e); }
try { A(); } catch (IOException e) { LogA(e); } try { B(); } catch (IOException e) { LogB(e); }
try { A(); } catch (IOException e) { Log(e); } try { B(); } catch (ArgumentException e) { Log(e); }
